notes: Twenty-fifth validated clue batch compiled from official school websites.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: Its mission says the school will educate, equip, and empower each scholar to excel academically by providing equitable learning opportunities and collaborating with families and communities.
Official materials say the school was founded in 1997 by local business leaders.
The school uses the four-part framing, "Educate. Equip. Empower. Excel."
Official materials highlight a whole-child model with supports such as food security, health and wellness, wrap-around services, and daily SEL.
Official staff pages list Dr. Heather Collins as head of school and director of operations.
Official board pages list Keen Gravely as chairman.
Its mission says the school provides high-quality education through academics, technology, and community involvement, with the integration of American Indian culture.
Official materials say the school wants students to become positive, productive citizens in an ever-changing global society.
The school highlights programs such as Indian Education and Mighty Bucks Academy.
A principal's message says students are encouraged to share the strength of their history to inspire others.
Official homepage materials identify Melissa Richardson as principal and head administrator.
Official board pages list Gideon Lee as chair and Dr. B. Ogletree Richardson as chief.
Its mission says the school provides a high-quality education preparing today's students for tomorrow's world while focusing on regional agriculture and related industries.
Official materials say the school wants students to become respectful and productive citizens.
The school says it is growing students academically, socially, and emotionally.
Official materials frame the school around service to the Pocosin region and its industries.
Official board pages list Stephanie Phelps as board chairman.
Its board page says meetings are held on the third Tuesday of each month at 6:00 p.m.
Its mission says the school seeks to nurture every student, every day to develop strong character, academic excellence, and good citizenship.
Official materials say the school was established in 2001.
The school's vision says, "Every child will learn. Every kid. Every day. No excuses."
Official materials say the campus relocated to a former manufacturing facility on land bordering the Catawba River.
Official pages list Lisa Ervin as director.
Official board pages list Jamie Smith as board chair.

Its mission says the school is a vibrant learning community where diverse students build strong academic foundations, executive function skills, and trust in their abilities for lives of joy and purpose.
Official materials say the school provides a quality education rooted in the Montessori method.
The school emphasizes hands-on learning, collaborative work, and self-directed activity as core Montessori features.
A campus story says the school first welcomed students in the fall of 2018.
Official team pages list Katherine Rucker as founder and head of school.
Official board pages list Ronda Hawkins as board chair.
